title: quercus × sterrettii
intro: Quercus × sterrettii, also known as Sterrett's oak, is a hybrid tree species resulting from the cross between Quercus coccinea (scarlet oak) and Quercus alba (white oak). The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its potential medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Quercus × sterrettii are attributed to its ability to support cardiovascular health, boost the immune system, and exhibit anti-cancer properties. Herbal preparations of Quercus × sterrettii, such as tinctures and infusions, can be used to harness its medicinal properties, although more research is needed to fully understand its effects on human health.
